description_long: This object is a vintage cathode ray tube (CRT) television with a rectangular wooden cabinet and a prominent convex glass screen. The front features a metallic bezel framing the screen, with a control panel on the right side that includes two large rotary dials and several push buttons below. The top and sides are finished in a wood veneer, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance, while the back is a protruding black plastic housing for the electronics and picture tube. The television sits on a flat black base, providing stability. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of analog televisions from the 1970s and 1980s.
description: A vintage CRT television with a wood veneer cabinet, convex glass screen, and metallic control panel featuring rotary dials and buttons.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wood veneer panel and the depth of the black plastic back housing.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the convex glass screen, metallic bezel, and control panel with rotary dials and buttons.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the wood veneer and the extended black plastic back.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the large black plastic housing and ventilation slots.
attribute_bezel_design: Medium-thickness metallic bezel, silver color, rectangular with rounded corners, classic vintage styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and push buttons on the front right side panel.
